sections.title: A: Complete using the verbs in the box
sections.instructions: Use the verbs in the box: apologise, defend, introduce, recognise, rent, respect, trust.

items.sentence:
'Who's that over there?'
'That's Graham Western, the actor. Let me ...... you.'

'Hi, Harry!'
'Oh! Hi, Rita! I didn't ...... you with your new hair style!'

'I'm really sorry!'
'There's no need to ...... .'

'I'm thinking of moving house soon.'
'Do you want to buy or ...... a place?'

'I wish I hadn't told Rebecca some of my secrets.'
'Don't worry. You can ...... Rebecca. She won't tell anyone.'

'Mr Parker is going to run a marathon for charity.'
'Yes, I really ...... Mr Parker. He does such a lot of charity work.'

'I'm sure Billy didn't say that!'
'Why do you always ...... Billy? He's not perfect, you know!'

sections.title: B: Write one word in each gap
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: Could you look ...... our rabbit while we're on holiday?
Tim was Sandy's boyfriend, but they split ...... last month.
Kim and Katy have fallen ...... with each other, so Kim isn't going to invite Katy to her party.
Phil was brought ...... by his uncle and aunt.
Is Gareth really going ...... with Liz?

sections.title: C: Word formation
sections.instructions: Complete by changing the form of the word in capitals.

items.sentence: I've got so much ...... for Darren. (ADMIRE)
Susie is so ...... . She's always breaking things! (CARE)
Tony said his dad is a millionaire, but he's such a ...... . (LIE)
Lots of ...... people live full and happy lives. (ABLE)
Karen apologised, so I ...... her immediately. (FORGIVE)
Andrea has got a great ...... . You'll really like her! (PERSON)
I'm not sure I've got the ...... to sing in public! (CONFIDENT)
I hate people who are ...... . (HONEST)
